summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of the 9/26/72 Stanley Theater show highlights a smoking “He’s Gone”, an unrivaled Baby Blue and a monster Truckin’ → The Other One jam.
review: The recording is a superb soundboard source that delivers studio‑level clarity, despite a brief tape‑change gap in the Truckin'→Other One segment. Musically the concert shines with a blistering “He’s Gone”, an unforgettable Baby Blue and tight jams on Mississippi Halfstep and Bird Song, though a few numbers suffer from out‑of‑tune guitars. Overall the performance is celebrated by fans as one of the high points of the legendary September ’72 run.

ai_rating.rationale: Average fan rating ~4.7, most reviewers praise both the performance and the pristine soundboard quality; only minor tuning issues prevent a perfect score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix with studio‑like fidelity. One reviewer notes a missing few minutes from the Truckin'→Other One jam due to a tape change, but no distortion or noise issues.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set lifts off with high energy and strong tunes; the second set builds into extended jams, highlighted by the Truckin'→Other One sequence and a soaring Baby Blue finale. The only blemish is a brief missing bass solo in the Other One due to a tape change.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for crisp vocals and guitar on Baby Blue; delivers an inspired lead (possible pedal‑steel) around 9:40, though some guitars run out of tune later.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass work lauded, especially the jam in The Other One, but a bass solo is missing because of the tape change.
band_member_comments.Bob: Rhythm guitar holds up the band; occasional out‑of‑tune moments (e.g., El Paso) noted.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keyboard (piano/organ) sits low in the mix but adds tasteful color; Keith’s influence felt throughout.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drum performance highlighted as a standout element, driving the jams.
